task_completion.description: The agent places a ruler tool to measure the maximum diameter of the tumor on the axial slice.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the medical viewer (e.g., 3D Slicer), a measurement line/ruler is visible spanning the longest dimension of the tumor on the axial (top-down) view.

task_completion.description: The agent places a second ruler perpendicular to the first to complete the bidimensional measurement.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second measurement line is visible on the axial view, crossing the first measurement line at approximately a 90-degree angle within the tumor boundaries.

task_completion.description: The agent navigates to or configures the viewer to show the tumor in Sagittal and Coronal planes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows the medical viewer displaying Sagittal (side view) and Coronal (front view) slices centered on the tumor anatomy.

task_completion.description: The agent adds a text annotation labeling the finding on the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text label (e.g., 'Brain tumor' or similar) is visible overlaid on one of the slice views near the tumor.

task_completion.description: The agent creates the JSON report containing the measured values and required fields.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal is visible showing the creation or editing of 'documentation_report.json' with fields like 'max_axial_diameter_mm' and 'tumor_location'.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the required screenshots and markup files to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, file explorer, or terminal listing shows the existence or saving of 'axial_view.png', 'measurements.mrk.json', and other required filenames in '~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/Documentation/'.

integrity.description: The agent uses the medical imaging software GUI to perform measurements rather than purely scripting or guessing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the application interface (e.g., mouse cursor on tools, active ruler placement) rather than only using a terminal/code to generate outputs.

integrity.description: The values recorded in the report appear derived from the actual GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: The numbers entered into the JSON report (e.g., diameter in mm) roughly correspond to the values displayed on the measurement rulers in the GUI screenshots.

integrity.description: The agent does not simply paste a pre-written report without performing the steps.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen performing the navigation and measurement steps before the report file is created or populated.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The BraTS dataset labels are: 0=background, 1=necrotic tumor core (NCR), 2=peritumoral edematous/invaded tissue (ED), 4=GD-enhancing tumor (ET). The 'tumor' finding generally encompasses the regions labeled 1, 2, and 4. The VLM should expect the measurements to span the entire abnormal region (edema + core + enhancing).
